_comment: Claude/Codex-parity policy set 2026-07-09 night by manager per Chris: 50% Anthropic / 50% OpenAI / 0% Cursor, superseding the 2026-07-06 Codex-primary (95/5) policy. Provenance: agent-platform/output/2026-07-09-overnight-5050-admissible-wave.md ('Trigger: direct order from Chris') and agent-platform/output/2026-07-10-morning-refuel-5050-wave.md ('Split ... 50/50 as directed'). This file was stale (still 95/5) for ~9h after the directive purely because nobody had edited it yet -- investigated per cto/output/2026-07-10-model-policy-5050-watcher-investigation.md and confirmed NO active watcher/generator writes this file today (every in-repo consumer, src/model-policy/policy.ts and src/agent-manager-db.ts, only reads it; grepped src/ and scripts/ for writers, found none). The 'prior watcher' warning below refers to a resolved 2026-06-25 cursor-conduit incident, not a live mechanism -- kept as a standing caution, not a current threat. This is still a manual hotfix, not the durable fix: model-policy.json and the per-agent live runtime can independently drift (exactly this incident). The durable fix is Slice 1 (Runtime Policy Schema and Persistence) of agent-platform/output/2026-07-09-runtime-mix-capacity-control.md, already CTO-architecture-approved in cto/output/2026-07-09-kg05-runtime-resilience-cto-guidance.md -- do not re-scope; extend that. Standing rule: new unpinned scheduler-created implementation work has no single deterministic preference under an even split (see NOTE on 'default' below); named-agent primary/fallback here reflects each agent's CURRENT confirmed live runtime per the wave docs above, not a policy preference. Cursor disabled for now (constrained_providers). NOTE: a prior watcher (2026-06-25 cursor-conduit bedtime watch, since removed from the codebase) reverted this file once historically; if policy drifts again, re-run the investigation above before assuming a watcher is the cause -- last time it was simply a stale manual edit.
